articles.headline: Government Launches 'Bharat Digital Connect' Initiative to Bridge Rural-Urban Digital Divide
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today unveiled the 'Bharat Digital Connect' initiative, a comprehensive program aimed at universal digital inclusion.
• The initiative focuses on expanding high-speed internet connectivity to all gram panchayats and remote villages by 2028.
• It integrates efforts from BharatNet, Common Service Centres (CSCs), and digital literacy programs under a single umbrella.
• Key components include subsidized device distribution, local content creation, and skill development for digital services.
• Aims to leverage India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) to empower rural economies and enhance access to government services.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Development processes and the development industry
Role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
prelimsPoints: 'Bharat Digital Connect' aims for universal digital inclusion by 2028.
Integrates BharatNet, CSCs, and digital literacy programs.
Focus on high-speed internet to gram panchayats and remote villages.
Utilizes India's DPI framework.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of digital inclusion for socio-economic development in India.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in bridging the rural-urban digital divide.
Evaluate the potential impact of 'Bharat Digital Connect' on e-governance, financial inclusion, and education.
Examine the role of DPI in achieving developmental goals.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration depicting digital connectivity reaching rural India, with people accessing services on devices.
articles.trivia: India has the world's largest number of internet users after China, with significant growth still coming from rural areas.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate, Reiterates Focus on Inflation Targeting Amid Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized the MPC's commitment to bringing inflation within the target band of 2-6%, with a medium-term target of 4%.
• The decision comes against a backdrop of persistent global supply chain disruptions and volatile crude oil prices, posing upside risks to inflation.
• RBI revised its FY26 GDP growth projection slightly downwards to 6.8% from 7.0%, citing global slowdown concerns.
• Liquidity management measures will continue to ensure adequate credit flow to productive sectors while managing inflationary pressures.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate remains unchanged at 6.5%.
RBI's inflation target is 2-6%, with a medium-term goal of 4%.
FY26 GDP growth projection revised to 6.8%.
MPC considers global supply chain and crude oil volatility.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflation.
Evaluate the impact of global economic headwinds on India's monetary policy decisions and growth outlook.
Examine the role of the MPC in maintaining financial stability.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with its logo, symbolizing monetary policy decisions.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India-US Strategic Dialogue Concludes with Focus on Critical Technologies, Defense Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-US Strategic Dialogue concluded in Washington D.C., co-chaired by External Affairs Minister and Secretary of State.
• Key outcomes include enhanced cooperation in critical and emerging technologies, particularly in AI, quantum computing, and semiconductors.
• Discussions also focused on strengthening defense partnerships, including co-production and co-development of military hardware.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and discussed regional security challenges.
• Agreements were signed on cybersecurity collaboration and facilitating technology transfer for dual-use applications.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: India-US Strategic Dialogue held in Washington D.C.
Focus on AI, quantum computing, semiconductors.
Strengthening defense co-production and co-development.
Commitment to a free Indo-Pacific.
Agreements on cybersecurity and technology transfer.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving dynamics of India-US strategic partnership, particularly in the context of critical technologies and defense.
Discuss the significance of the Indo-Pacific region in India's foreign policy and its alignment with US objectives.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in diversifying its defense procurement and enhancing indigenous capabilities through international collaborations.
Examine the role of technology partnerships in shaping geopolitical alliances.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United States displayed together, symbolizing diplomatic relations.
articles.trivia: The '2+2' Ministerial Dialogue is another significant format for high-level engagement between India and the US, involving their respective Foreign and Defense Ministers.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reserves Verdict on Electoral Bonds Transparency, Hearing Concludes
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today concluded hearings and reserved its verdict on a batch of petitions challenging the Electoral Bonds Scheme.
• Petitioners argued that the scheme compromises electoral transparency and anonymous funding leads to quid pro quo arrangements.
• The government defended the scheme, citing donor privacy and curbing black money in political funding as primary objectives.
• The five-judge Constitution Bench heard arguments on fundamental rights, including the right to information and voter's right to know.
• The outcome is expected to have significant implications for political funding and electoral reforms in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court reserved verdict on Electoral Bonds Scheme.
Petitions challenge scheme on transparency grounds.
Government cites donor privacy and curbing black money.
Five-judge Constitution Bench heard the case.
Impacts political funding and electoral reforms.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds Scheme in India.
Discuss the importance of transparency in political funding for a healthy democracy.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in upholding electoral integrity and fundamental rights related to information.
Suggest potential reforms to enhance transparency in political financing.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building, symbolizing judicial scrutiny on a crucial national issue.
articles.trivia: The first electoral bonds were issued in March 2018. They are valid for fifteen days from the date of issue.

articles.headline: Union Cabinet Approves New National Policy on Sustainable Urban Planning and Development
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet today approved the 'National Policy on Sustainable Urban Planning and Development (NPSUPD) 2025'.
• The policy aims to promote green building standards, efficient public transport systems, and integrated waste management in urban areas.
• It mandates the adoption of climate-resilient infrastructure and encourages the use of renewable energy in urban projects.
• Emphasis is placed on citizen participation in urban governance and leveraging technology for smart city solutions.
• The policy targets reducing the carbon footprint of Indian cities by 20% by 2035 and enhancing liveability scores.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: NPSUPD 2025 approved by Union Cabinet.
Aims for green building, public transport, waste management.
Mandates climate-resilient infrastructure.
Targets 20% carbon footprint reduction by 2035.
Emphasizes citizen participation and smart city tech.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges of rapid urbanization in India and the need for sustainable urban planning.
Discuss the key features of the NPSUPD 2025 and its potential to transform Indian cities.
Evaluate the role of technology and citizen participation in achieving sustainable urban development goals.
Examine the linkages between urban planning, climate change mitigation, and economic growth.
articles.imageDescription: Modern cityscape with green spaces and sustainable transport, representing smart urban planning.
articles.trivia: By 2050, it is estimated that India's urban population will grow by an additional 400 million people, making sustainable planning crucial.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Next-Gen Satellite for Enhanced Disaster Management and Early Warning
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched a new-generation geo-imaging satellite, 'EOS-08' (Earth Observation Satellite-08).
• EOS-08 is equipped with advanced synthetic aperture radar (SAR) and high-resolution optical sensors for precise data collection.
• It will significantly enhance India's capabilities in disaster management, providing real-time data for flood mapping, cyclone tracking, and landslide monitoring.
• The satellite is also designed for agricultural assessment, urban planning, and coastal zone management.
• This mission underscores India's growing self-reliance in space technology and its commitment to utilizing space assets for societal benefit.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: ISRO launched 'EOS-08'.
EOS-08 is a geo-imaging satellite.
Equipped with SAR and optical sensors.
Enhances disaster management, flood mapping, cyclone tracking.
Also used for agriculture, urban planning, coastal zone management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of space technology in disaster management and mitigation efforts in India.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in remote sensing contribute to various sectors like agriculture and urban development.
Evaluate India's progress in achieving self-reliance in space technology and its geopolitical implications.
Examine the challenges in integrating satellite data into ground-level disaster response mechanisms.
articles.imageDescription: An ISRO rocket launching into space, with the Earth visible in the background, symbolizing satellite deployment.
articles.trivia: India's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on April 19, 1975, by the Soviet Union.

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission: Progress Report Highlights Key Achievements and Future Roadmap
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy released a progress report on the National Green Hydrogen Mission, highlighting significant strides.
• India has attracted over $5 billion in investments for green hydrogen projects, primarily in Gujarat and Karnataka.
• The report notes the successful commissioning of pilot projects for green hydrogen production and its application in industrial sectors like refineries.
• Challenges remain in reducing the cost of green hydrogen production and developing robust storage and transportation infrastructure.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030, targeting 5 million tonnes per annum capacity.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission progress report released.
Attracted over $5 billion investment.
Pilot projects commissioned in Gujarat, Karnataka.
Challenges: cost reduction, storage, transport.
Aim: 5 MTPA capacity by 2030, India as global hub.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic viability and technological challenges associated with scaling up green hydrogen production.
Evaluate the potential of the National Green Hydrogen Mission to transform India into an export hub for green energy.
Examine the policy support and investment required to achieve the mission's ambitious targets.
articles.imageDescription: Stylized image of a hydrogen molecule forming from green energy sources like wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all normal matter.

articles.headline: Comprehensive Border Management System (CBMS) Deployment in Sensitive Sectors Shows Positive Results
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs announced the successful deployment of the Comprehensive Border Management System (CBMS) in several sensitive border sectors.
• CBMS integrates advanced surveillance technologies, including drones, thermal imagers, ground sensors, and real-time data analytics.
• The system aims to enhance border security, prevent infiltration, and curb smuggling activities more effectively.
• Initial reports indicate a significant reduction in cross-border incidents and improved response times for border guarding forces.
• The government plans to expand CBMS coverage to all critical land and coastal borders over the next five years.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: CBMS deployed in sensitive border sectors.
Integrates drones, thermal imagers, ground sensors.
Aims to enhance border security and prevent infiltration.
Initial reports show reduction in cross-border incidents.
Planned expansion to all critical borders.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of border security challenges for India and the need for technological solutions.
Analyze the components and effectiveness of the Comprehensive Border Management System (CBMS).
Evaluate the balance between technology deployment and human intelligence in securing India's borders.
Examine the geopolitical implications of enhanced border security on regional stability.
articles.imageDescription: High-tech border fence with surveillance cameras and drones patrolling, symbolizing modern border management.
articles.trivia: India has over 15,000 km of land border and 7,500 km of coastline, making border management a complex task.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Mid-Term Review Highlights Implementation Progress and Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a mid-term review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report notes significant progress in curriculum reforms, particularly the integration of vocational education and multidisciplinary approaches.
• Challenges persist in teacher training, infrastructure development, and ensuring equitable access to quality education, especially in rural areas.
• The Higher Education Commission of India (HECI) framework is progressing, aiming to streamline regulatory bodies.
• The review emphasizes the need for continued investment and collaborative efforts from states to achieve NEP's transformative goals.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Mid-term review of NEP 2020 released.
Progress in curriculum reforms, vocational education.
Challenges in teacher training, infrastructure, equitable access.
HECI framework progressing.
Emphasizes state collaboration and investment.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the progress and challenges in implementing the National Education Policy 2020.
Discuss how NEP 2020 aims to address the skill gap and promote multidisciplinary learning.
Analyze the role of federalism in the successful implementation of national education policies.
Suggest measures to overcome the existing barriers to achieving universal quality education in India.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing educational reforms and learning.
articles.trivia: The first National Policy on Education in India was promulgated in 1968, followed by another in 1986.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Proposes National AI Ethics Framework for Responsible AI Development
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog today released a discussion paper proposing a comprehensive National AI Ethics Framework for India.
• The framework emphasizes principles of fairness, accountability, transparency, safety, and privacy in the development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence.
• It aims to foster responsible AI innovation while mitigating potential risks such as bias, discrimination, and job displacement.
• The framework suggests a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and civil society for its implementation.
• This move positions India as a leader in developing ethical guidelines for AI, balancing technological progress with societal well-being.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog proposed National AI Ethics Framework.
Emphasizes fairness, accountability, transparency, safety, privacy.
Aims for responsible AI innovation and risk mitigation.
Suggests multi-stakeholder approach.
Positions India as a leader in AI ethics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical dilemmas and societal implications posed by the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence.
Analyze the key principles of NITI Aayog's proposed AI Ethics Framework and its relevance in the Indian context.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a comprehensive AI ethics framework, considering technological advancements and diverse stakeholder interests.
Examine how India can balance AI innovation with ethical governance to ensure inclusive growth.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract image showing a human brain connected to digital circuits, symbolizing AI and ethical considerations.
articles.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined in 1956 by John McCarthy at the Dartmouth Conference.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Free Trade Agreement (FTA) Review Committee Submits Recommendations for Modernization
articles.explainer:
• The joint committee reviewing the India-ASEAN Free Trade Agreement (FTA) in Goods submitted its recommendations for modernization.
• The review aims to make the FTA more user-friendly, transparent, and trade-facilitative by addressing non-tariff barriers and streamlining customs procedures.
• Key recommendations include expanding product coverage, reducing tariffs on certain sensitive items, and integrating digital trade provisions.
• The modernization is expected to boost bilateral trade and investment between India and ASEAN member states, which currently stands at over $100 billion.
• This move is part of India's 'Act East' policy, deepening economic integration with Southeast Asia.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
India and its neighborhood- relations
prelimsPoints: India-ASEAN FTA review committee submitted recommendations.
Aims for modernization, user-friendliness, transparency.
Recommendations include expanding product coverage, reducing tariffs, digital trade.
Expected to boost bilateral trade over $100 billion.
Part of India's 'Act East' policy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-ASEAN Free Trade Agreement for India's economic and strategic interests in Southeast Asia.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in modernizing existing FTAs to address contemporary trade dynamics, including digital trade.
Evaluate the 'Act East' policy's success in deepening India's engagement with ASEAN nations.
Examine how addressing non-tariff barriers can significantly enhance trade flows between member countries.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states arranged together, symbolizing trade partnership.
articles.trivia: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) was established on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand.

articles.headline: Coastal Zone Management Plan Implementation Reviewed: Focus on Resilience and Livelihoods
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change reviewed the progress of Coastal Zone Management Plans (CZMPs) implementation.
• The review highlighted efforts in protecting critical coastal ecosystems like mangroves and coral reefs, and mitigating coastal erosion.
• Challenges include balancing developmental activities with environmental conservation and ensuring the participation of local fishing communities.
• New guidelines are proposed to integrate climate change adaptation measures, such as seawalls and early warning systems, into CZMPs.
• The plans aim to safeguard coastal livelihoods, promote sustainable tourism, and enhance disaster preparedness in coastal regions.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: CZMPs implementation reviewed by MoEFCC.
Focus on protecting mangroves, coral reefs, mitigating erosion.
Challenges: balancing development and conservation, community participation.
New guidelines for climate change adaptation.
Aims to safeguard livelihoods, promote sustainable tourism, disaster preparedness.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of India's coastal zones.
Analyze the challenges in implementing Coastal Zone Management Plans, particularly regarding stakeholder conflicts and climate change impacts.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current policies in protecting coastal ecosystems and ensuring sustainable livelihoods.
Suggest measures for enhancing coastal resilience and integrating local communities in conservation efforts.
articles.imageDescription: A healthy mangrove forest along a coastline, illustrating coastal ecosystem protection.
articles.trivia: The Sundarbans, shared between India and Bangladesh, is the largest single block of tidal halophytic mangrove forest in the world.

articles.headline: India on Track to Exceed 2030 Renewable Energy Target, Ministry Report Claims
articles.explainer:
• A report by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy indicates that India is well on its way to exceeding its 2030 renewable energy targets.
• The country has already achieved over 180 GW of installed renewable capacity, including large hydro, surpassing initial projections.
• Aggressive solar and wind energy deployment, coupled with policy incentives, have been key drivers of this growth.
• The report highlights the role of schemes like the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) for solar PV manufacturing in boosting domestic production.
• While grid integration and storage remain challenges, India is poised to become a global leader in renewable energy transition.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: India on track to exceed 2030 renewable energy targets.
Over 180 GW installed renewable capacity achieved.
Solar and wind deployment, policy incentives are key drivers.
PLI scheme for solar PV manufacturing boosting domestic production.
Challenges: grid integration, storage.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's progress in renewable energy adoption and its implications for energy security and climate change mitigation.
Analyze the effectiveness of government policies and schemes, such as PLI, in accelerating the renewable energy transition.
Evaluate the challenges associated with integrating high shares of renewable energy into the national grid and potential solutions.
Examine India's potential to emerge as a global leader in renewable energy technology and deployment.
articles.imageDescription: Vast solar panel farm stretching into the horizon, symbolizing renewable energy generation.
articles.trivia: The International Solar Alliance (ISA) was conceived as a joint effort by India and France to mobilize efforts against climate change through solar energy.

articles.headline: India Calls for Enhanced Global Cooperation at G20 Summit on Economic Resilience
articles.explainer:
• India's Prime Minister addressed the G20 Summit, emphasizing the need for enhanced global cooperation to build economic resilience.
• He advocated for reforms in multilateral financial institutions to ensure equitable representation and responsiveness to developing nations' needs.
• Discussions focused on addressing global debt vulnerabilities, ensuring food and energy security, and promoting sustainable development goals.
• India highlighted its successful digital public infrastructure model as a template for other nations to foster inclusive economic growth.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration reinforcing commitments to global stability and addressing shared challenges.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: PM addressed G20 Summit on economic resilience.
Advocated reforms in multilateral financial institutions.
Discussions on global debt, food/energy security, SDGs.
India highlighted DPI model for inclusive growth.
Joint declaration on global stability.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's growing role and influence in global economic governance forums like the G20.
Analyze the necessity of reforming multilateral financial institutions to address current global economic challenges effectively.
Evaluate the potential of India's Digital Public Infrastructure model as a tool for fostering inclusive economic growth globally.
Examine the interconnectedness of global economic resilience with food security, energy security, and sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders of G20 nations seated around a large table, symbolizing global diplomacy and cooperation.
articles.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis.

articles.headline: Mental Health Care Act 2017: Independent Evaluation Calls for Stronger Implementation
articles.explainer:
• An independent evaluation report on the Mental Health Care Act (MHCA) 2017 was released, assessing its five-year implementation.
• The report commends the Act's progressive shift towards rights-based mental healthcare but highlights gaps in implementation.
• Key challenges include inadequate funding, shortage of trained professionals, and lack of awareness about patient rights.
• It recommends strengthening community-based mental health services and integrating mental health into primary healthcare.
• The evaluation underscores the need for robust grievance redressal mechanisms and greater public expenditure on mental health infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Independent evaluation of MHCA 2017 released.
Commends rights-based approach, highlights implementation gaps.
Challenges: inadequate funding, professional shortage, lack of awareness.
Recommendations: strengthen community services, integrate into primary healthcare.
Calls for robust grievance redressal, public expenditure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the shift from a custodial to a rights-based approach in mental healthcare in India, as enshrined in the MHCA 2017.
Analyze the barriers to effective implementation of mental health policies in India, including funding and human resource challenges.
Evaluate the importance of community-based mental health services and integration with primary healthcare.
Suggest policy measures to improve mental health outcomes and reduce stigma in Indian society.
articles.imageDescription: A silhouette of a person with a thoughtful expression, symbolizing mental well-being and support.
articles.trivia: Globally, close to 1 billion people live with a mental disorder, and 3 million people die every year from the harmful use of alcohol.

articles.headline: Government to Launch Phase 2 of Revamped PM-KISAN Scheme with Enhanced Benefits
articles.explainer:
• The Union Agriculture Ministry announced plans to launch Phase 2 of the revamped Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme.
• The second phase will introduce enhanced financial assistance for small and marginal farmers, including linkage with crop insurance and credit facilities.
• It aims to streamline beneficiary identification through Aadhaar and land record integration, minimizing leakages and ensuring timely disbursals.
• The revamped scheme will also focus on promoting sustainable agricultural practices and farmer producer organizations (FPOs).
• This initiative is expected to further boost farmer incomes and contribute to agricultural sector resilience.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country
Different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
E-technology in the aid of farmers
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
prelimsPoints: Phase 2 of revamped PM-KISAN scheme to be launched.
Enhanced financial assistance, linkage with crop insurance, credit.
Streamlined beneficiary identification via Aadhaar and land records.
Focus on sustainable agriculture and FPOs.
Aims to boost farmer incomes and resilience.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and impact of the PM-KISAN scheme on farmer welfare and agricultural productivity in India.
Analyze the effectiveness of direct benefit transfers (DBT) in reducing leakages and improving last-mile delivery of agricultural subsidies.
Evaluate the role of technology (Aadhaar, land records) in enhancing the efficiency and transparency of farmer support schemes.
Examine how linking financial assistance with sustainable practices and FPOs can transform the agricultural sector.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer working in a field with modern agricultural tools, symbolizing farmer welfare schemes.
articles.trivia: PM-KISAN was launched on 24 February 2019, providing income support to all eligible farmer families across the country.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Bilateral Talks on Indus Waters Treaty, Emphasize Technical Resolution
articles.explainer:
• Delegations from India and Pakistan held bilateral talks on issues related to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in New Delhi.
• The discussions focused on resolving technical differences over the design of hydroelectric projects being constructed by India on the Chenab river tributaries.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to the IWT and the established dispute resolution mechanisms under the treaty.
• While no major breakthroughs were announced, the talks were described as constructive, aiming for amicable solutions.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, governs the sharing of waters of the Indus River system.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Pakistan held talks on Indus Waters Treaty.
Discussions on technical differences over Chenab projects.
Both sides committed to IWT and dispute resolution.
Talks described as constructive.
IWT brokered by World Bank in 1960.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Indus Waters Treaty in India-Pakistan relations and its role in regional water sharing.
Analyze the challenges and complexities involved in resolving technical disputes under the IWT framework.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of water disputes in South Asia and the importance of bilateral dialogue.
Examine the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River system and its tributaries flowing through India and Pakistan.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is often cited as one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, surviving multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: PM Vishwakarma Scheme to Expand Reach, Include More Traditional Artisans
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship announced the expansion of the PM Vishwakarma Kaushal Samman (PM-VIKAS) scheme.
• The scheme, aimed at traditional artisans and craftspeople, will now include additional trades and offer enhanced financial support and skill training.
• Focus will be on integrating Vishwakarma beneficiaries into the digital economy and providing market linkages for their products.
• The expansion seeks to preserve India's rich artisanal heritage while promoting entrepreneurship and self-employment.
• Training modules will incorporate modern techniques alongside traditional skills, ensuring relevance in contemporary markets.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Skill Development
prelimsPoints: PM Vishwakarma scheme to expand.
Include more trades, enhanced financial support, skill training.
Focus on digital economy integration, market linkages.
Aims to preserve heritage, promote entrepreneurship.
Training to blend modern and traditional techniques.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of supporting traditional artisans and craftspeople for cultural preservation and economic empowerment in India.
Analyze the objectives and impact of the PM Vishwakarma scheme in promoting skill development and entrepreneurship.
Evaluate the challenges faced by traditional artisans in a modern economy and how policy interventions can address them.
Examine the role of market linkages and digital integration in making artisanal products competitive globally.
articles.imageDescription: An Indian artisan meticulously crafting a traditional item, symbolizing heritage and skill.
articles.trivia: India's handicraft sector is one of the largest employment generators after agriculture, supporting millions of artisans.

articles.headline: New Foreign Trade Policy Unveiled, Aims to Boost Exports to $2 Trillion by 2030
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry unveiled the new Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2025-30, replacing the previous policy.
• The policy sets an ambitious target of achieving $2 trillion in exports of goods and services by 2030.
• Key features include simplified procedures, automation of processes, and a focus on emerging sectors like green energy and e-commerce exports.
• It introduces new incentive schemes and rationalizes existing ones to promote 'Make in India' products globally.
• The FTP also emphasizes district-as-export-hubs initiative and strengthening India's position in global value chains.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: New Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2025-30 unveiled.
Target: $2 trillion exports by 2030.
Features: simplified procedures, automation, focus on green energy, e-commerce.
New incentive schemes, rationalized existing ones.
Emphasizes 'district-as-export-hubs' and global value chains.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and key features of India's new Foreign Trade Policy 2025-30.
Discuss the strategies outlined in the policy to achieve the ambitious export target of $2 trillion by 2030.
Evaluate the potential impact of promoting 'Make in India' products and strengthening global value chains on India's economic growth.
Examine the role of e-commerce and district-level initiatives in diversifying and boosting India's export basket.
articles.imageDescription: A container ship loading at a port, with global trade routes overlaid, symbolizing international commerce.
articles.trivia: India's merchandise exports crossed the $400 billion mark for the first time in FY22.

highlights: Government launches 'Bharat Digital Connect' to bridge rural-urban digital divide, leveraging DPI for universal inclusion by 2028.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5%, reaffirming commitment to inflation targeting amidst global economic uncertainties.
India-US Strategic Dialogue concludes, focusing on enhanced cooperation in critical technologies like AI and semiconductors, and defense.
Supreme Court reserves verdict on Electoral Bonds Scheme, a decision expected to significantly impact political funding transparency.
Union Cabinet approves 'National Policy on Sustainable Urban Planning and Development 2025' to promote green and climate-resilient cities.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements regarding the 'Bharat Digital Connect' initiative is/are correct?
1. It aims to provide high-speed internet connectivity to all gram panchayats and remote villages by 2028.
2. It integrates efforts from BharatNet, Common Service Centres (CSCs), and digital literacy programs.
3. The initiative primarily focuses on urban digital transformation.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The initiative aims to expand high-speed internet connectivity to all gram panchayats and remote villages by 2028. Statement 2 is correct: It integrates efforts from BharatNet, Common Service Centres (CSCs), and digital literacy programs. Statement 3 is incorrect: The initiative's primary focus is on bridging the rural-urban digital divide, not primarily urban transformation.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements about the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy:
1. The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is primarily responsible for deciding the repo rate.
2. RBI's medium-term inflation target is 4%, with a band of +/- 2%.
3. A reduction in the repo rate typically makes borrowings cheaper for banks, potentially boosting economic activity.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The MPC determines the policy repo rate. Statement 2 is correct: RBI's medium-term inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2% (i.e., 2-6%). Statement 3 is correct: A lower repo rate reduces the cost at which banks borrow from the RBI, enabling them to lend at lower rates, which can stimulate economic activity.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'EOS-08' satellite recently launched by ISRO, consider the following statements:
1. It is a communication satellite designed for secure military communications.
2. It is equipped with Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) and high-resolution optical sensors.
3. Its applications include disaster management, agricultural assessment, and urban planning.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: EOS-08 is a geo-imaging/Earth Observation Satellite, not primarily a communication satellite for military use. Statement 2 is correct: It is equipped with advanced SAR and high-resolution optical sensors. Statement 3 is correct: Its key applications include disaster management, flood mapping, cyclone tracking, agricultural assessment, and urban planning.

prelimsMocks.question: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. What is the target capacity for green hydrogen production by 2030, as per the mission?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to achieve a green hydrogen production capacity of 5 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) by 2030, contributing to India's energy security and climate goals.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a primary objective of the new 'National Policy on Sustainable Urban Planning and Development (NPSUPD) 2025'?
options: (c) Centralizing all urban development projects under a single national agency.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Centralizing all urban development projects under a single national agency.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The NPSUPD 2025 focuses on promoting green building standards, climate-resilient infrastructure, and efficient public transport systems. It emphasizes citizen participation and leveraging technology for smart city solutions, but it does not primarily aim to centralize all urban development projects under a single national agency, which would be contrary to principles of decentralized governance often seen in urban planning.

mainsMocks.question: The launch of the 'Bharat Digital Connect' initiative signifies India's commitment to inclusive growth. Discuss the multi-faceted challenges in achieving universal digital inclusion in India and suggest policy measures to overcome them. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce 'Bharat Digital Connect' and its aim. Discuss challenges: infrastructure gaps (rural connectivity, last-mile), affordability of devices/data, digital literacy, language barriers, cybersecurity concerns. Suggest measures: expanding BharatNet, promoting domestic device manufacturing, targeted digital literacy programs, local content creation, public-private partnerships, regulatory framework for data protection, and leveraging CSCs effectively.

mainsMocks.question: India's pursuit of critical and emerging technologies (CET) is increasingly shaping its foreign policy and strategic partnerships. Analyze the significance of India-US cooperation in CET for India's strategic autonomy and economic development, particularly in the Indo-Pacific context. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Define CET and its importance (AI, semiconductors, quantum). Explain India's strategic autonomy goal. Discuss how US cooperation in CET (technology transfer, co-development, supply chain resilience) reduces dependence on other nations and boosts indigenous capabilities. Connect to economic development via innovation, job creation. Relate to Indo-Pacific security and balancing regional power dynamics. Conclude on mutual benefits and challenges.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's impending verdict on the Electoral Bonds Scheme holds significant implications for electoral transparency in India. Critically examine the arguments for and against the scheme, and discuss the importance of transparent political funding for a robust democracy. (10 marks, 150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act; Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
mainsMocks.approach: Briefly explain Electoral Bonds. Arguments for: donor privacy, curbing black money, formalizing donations. Arguments against: anonymity leads to quid pro quo, lack of transparency, impacts voter's right to know. Importance of transparency: accountability, level playing field, reducing corruption, strengthening democratic values. Conclude on the judiciary's role in upholding constitutional principles.

quickBytes.text: The 'Bharat Digital Connect' initiative aims for universal digital inclusion by 2028.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the repo rate to manage inflation and economic growth.
The Indus Waters Treaty (1960) governs water sharing between India and Pakistan, brokered by the World Bank.
EOS-08 is ISRO's new geo-imaging satellite for enhanced disaster management and Earth observation.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission targets 5 MTPA green hydrogen production by 2030.
Coastal Zone Management Plans (CZMPs) focus on protecting coastal ecosystems and livelihoods.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 promotes multidisciplinary education and vocational integration.
NITI Aayog proposed a National AI Ethics Framework for responsible AI development in India.
The PM-KISAN scheme provides income support to small and marginal farmer families.
India's new Foreign Trade Policy 2025-30 aims for $2 trillion exports by 2030.

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Bharat Digital Connect' Initiative to Bridge Rural-Urban Digital Divide
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government today unveiled the 'Bharat Digital Connect' initiative, a comprehensive program aimed at universal digital inclusion.
• The initiative focuses on expanding high-speed internet connectivity to all gram panchayats and remote villages by 2028.
• It integrates efforts from BharatNet, Common Service Centres (CSCs), and digital literacy programs under a single umbrella.
• Key components include subsidized device distribution, local content creation, and skill development for digital services.
• Aims to leverage India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) to empower rural economies and enhance access to government services.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Development processes and the development industry
Role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
prelimsPoints: 'Bharat Digital Connect' aims for universal digital inclusion by 2028.
Integrates BharatNet, CSCs, and digital literacy programs.
Focus on high-speed internet to gram panchayats and remote villages.
Utilizes India's DPI framework.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of digital inclusion for socio-economic development in India.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in bridging the rural-urban digital divide.
Evaluate the potential impact of 'Bharat Digital Connect' on e-governance, financial inclusion, and education.
Examine the role of DPI in achieving developmental goals.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration depicting digital connectivity reaching rural India, with people accessing services on devices.
GS2.trivia: India has the world's largest number of internet users after China, with significant growth still coming from rural areas.

GS2.headline: India-US Strategic Dialogue Concludes with Focus on Critical Technologies, Defense Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-US Strategic Dialogue concluded in Washington D.C., co-chaired by External Affairs Minister and Secretary of State.
• Key outcomes include enhanced cooperation in critical and emerging technologies, particularly in AI, quantum computing, and semiconductors.
• Discussions also focused on strengthening defense partnerships, including co-production and co-development of military hardware.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and discussed regional security challenges.
• Agreements were signed on cybersecurity collaboration and facilitating technology transfer for dual-use applications.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: India-US Strategic Dialogue held in Washington D.C.
Focus on AI, quantum computing, semiconductors.
Strengthening defense co-production and co-development.
Commitment to a free Indo-Pacific.
Agreements on cybersecurity and technology transfer.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving dynamics of India-US strategic partnership, particularly in the context of critical technologies and defense.
Discuss the significance of the Indo-Pacific region in India's foreign policy and its alignment with US objectives.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in diversifying its defense procurement and enhancing indigenous capabilities through international collaborations.
Examine the role of technology partnerships in shaping geopolitical alliances.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United States displayed together, symbolizing diplomatic relations.
GS2.trivia: The '2+2' Ministerial Dialogue is another significant format for high-level engagement between India and the US, involving their respective Foreign and Defense Ministers.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reserves Verdict on Electoral Bonds Transparency, Hearing Concludes
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today concluded hearings and reserved its verdict on a batch of petitions challenging the Electoral Bonds Scheme.
• Petitioners argued that the scheme compromises electoral transparency and anonymous funding leads to quid pro quo arrangements.
• The government defended the scheme, citing donor privacy and curbing black money in political funding as primary objectives.
• The five-judge Constitution Bench heard arguments on fundamental rights, including the right to information and voter's right to know.
• The outcome is expected to have significant implications for political funding and electoral reforms in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court reserved verdict on Electoral Bonds Scheme.
Petitions challenge scheme on transparency grounds.
Government cites donor privacy and curbing black money.
Five-judge Constitution Bench heard the case.
Impacts political funding and electoral reforms.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds Scheme in India.
Discuss the importance of transparency in political funding for a healthy democracy.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in upholding electoral integrity and fundamental rights related to information.
Suggest potential reforms to enhance transparency in political financing.
GS2.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building, symbolizing judicial scrutiny on a crucial national issue.
GS2.trivia: The first electoral bonds were issued in March 2018. They are valid for fifteen days from the date of issue.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Mid-Term Review Highlights Implementation Progress and Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a mid-term review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report notes significant progress in curriculum reforms, particularly the integration of vocational education and multidisciplinary approaches.
• Challenges persist in teacher training, infrastructure development, and ensuring equitable access to quality education, especially in rural areas.
• The Higher Education Commission of India (HECI) framework is progressing, aiming to streamline regulatory bodies.
• The review emphasizes the need for continued investment and collaborative efforts from states to achieve NEP's transformative goals.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Mid-term review of NEP 2020 released.
Progress in curriculum reforms, vocational education.
Challenges in teacher training, infrastructure, equitable access.
HECI framework progressing.
Emphasizes state collaboration and investment.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the progress and challenges in implementing the National Education Policy 2020.
Discuss how NEP 2020 aims to address the skill gap and promote multidisciplinary learning.
Analyze the role of federalism in the successful implementation of national education policies.
Suggest measures to overcome the existing barriers to achieving universal quality education in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing educational reforms and learning.
GS2.trivia: The first National Policy on Education in India was promulgated in 1968, followed by another in 1986.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Free Trade Agreement (FTA) Review Committee Submits Recommendations for Modernization
GS2.explainer:
• The joint committee reviewing the India-ASEAN Free Trade Agreement (FTA) in Goods submitted its recommendations for modernization.
• The review aims to make the FTA more user-friendly, transparent, and trade-facilitative by addressing non-tariff barriers and streamlining customs procedures.
• Key recommendations include expanding product coverage, reducing tariffs on certain sensitive items, and integrating digital trade provisions.
• The modernization is expected to boost bilateral trade and investment between India and ASEAN member states, which currently stands at over $100 billion.
• This move is part of India's 'Act East' policy, deepening economic integration with Southeast Asia.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
India and its neighborhood- relations
prelimsPoints: India-ASEAN FTA review committee submitted recommendations.
Aims for modernization, user-friendliness, transparency.
Recommendations include expanding product coverage, reducing tariffs, digital trade.
Expected to boost bilateral trade over $100 billion.
Part of India's 'Act East' policy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-ASEAN Free Trade Agreement for India's economic and strategic interests in Southeast Asia.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in modernizing existing FTAs to address contemporary trade dynamics, including digital trade.
Evaluate the 'Act East' policy's success in deepening India's engagement with ASEAN nations.
Examine how addressing non-tariff barriers can significantly enhance trade flows between member countries.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states arranged together, symbolizing trade partnership.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) was established on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand.

GS2.headline: India Calls for Enhanced Global Cooperation at G20 Summit on Economic Resilience
GS2.explainer:
• India's Prime Minister addressed the G20 Summit, emphasizing the need for enhanced global cooperation to build economic resilience.
• He advocated for reforms in multilateral financial institutions to ensure equitable representation and responsiveness to developing nations' needs.
• Discussions focused on addressing global debt vulnerabilities, ensuring food and energy security, and promoting sustainable development goals.
• India highlighted its successful digital public infrastructure model as a template for other nations to foster inclusive economic growth.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration reinforcing commitments to global stability and addressing shared challenges.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: PM addressed G20 Summit on economic resilience.
Advocated reforms in multilateral financial institutions.
Discussions on global debt, food/energy security, SDGs.
India highlighted DPI model for inclusive growth.
Joint declaration on global stability.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's growing role and influence in global economic governance forums like the G20.
Analyze the necessity of reforming multilateral financial institutions to address current global economic challenges effectively.
Evaluate the potential of India's Digital Public Infrastructure model as a tool for fostering inclusive economic growth globally.
Examine the interconnectedness of global economic resilience with food security, energy security, and sustainable development.
GS2.imageDescription: Leaders of G20 nations seated around a large table, symbolizing global diplomacy and cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis.

GS2.headline: Mental Health Care Act 2017: Independent Evaluation Calls for Stronger Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• An independent evaluation report on the Mental Health Care Act (MHCA) 2017 was released, assessing its five-year implementation.
• The report commends the Act's progressive shift towards rights-based mental healthcare but highlights gaps in implementation.
• Key challenges include inadequate funding, shortage of trained professionals, and lack of awareness about patient rights.
• It recommends strengthening community-based mental health services and integrating mental health into primary healthcare.
• The evaluation underscores the need for robust grievance redressal mechanisms and greater public expenditure on mental health infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Independent evaluation of MHCA 2017 released.
Commends rights-based approach, highlights implementation gaps.
Challenges: inadequate funding, professional shortage, lack of awareness.
Recommendations: strengthen community services, integrate into primary healthcare.
Calls for robust grievance redressal, public expenditure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the shift from a custodial to a rights-based approach in mental healthcare in India, as enshrined in the MHCA 2017.
Analyze the barriers to effective implementation of mental health policies in India, including funding and human resource challenges.
Evaluate the importance of community-based mental health services and integration with primary healthcare.
Suggest policy measures to improve mental health outcomes and reduce stigma in Indian society.
GS2.imageDescription: A silhouette of a person with a thoughtful expression, symbolizing mental well-being and support.
GS2.trivia: Globally, close to 1 billion people live with a mental disorder, and 3 million people die every year from the harmful use of alcohol.

GS2.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Bilateral Talks on Indus Waters Treaty, Emphasize Technical Resolution
GS2.explainer:
• Delegations from India and Pakistan held bilateral talks on issues related to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in New Delhi.
• The discussions focused on resolving technical differences over the design of hydroelectric projects being constructed by India on the Chenab river tributaries.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to the IWT and the established dispute resolution mechanisms under the treaty.
• While no major breakthroughs were announced, the talks were described as constructive, aiming for amicable solutions.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, governs the sharing of waters of the Indus River system.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-Pakistan held talks on Indus Waters Treaty.
Discussions on technical differences over Chenab projects.
Both sides committed to IWT and dispute resolution.
Talks described as constructive.
IWT brokered by World Bank in 1960.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Indus Waters Treaty in India-Pakistan relations and its role in regional water sharing.
Analyze the challenges and complexities involved in resolving technical disputes under the IWT framework.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of water disputes in South Asia and the importance of bilateral dialogue.
Examine the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River system and its tributaries flowing through India and Pakistan.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is often cited as one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, surviving multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

GS2.headline: PM Vishwakarma Scheme to Expand Reach, Include More Traditional Artisans
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship announced the expansion of the PM Vishwakarma Kaushal Samman (PM-VIKAS) scheme.
• The scheme, aimed at traditional artisans and craftspeople, will now include additional trades and offer enhanced financial support and skill training.
• Focus will be on integrating Vishwakarma beneficiaries into the digital economy and providing market linkages for their products.
• The expansion seeks to preserve India's rich artisanal heritage while promoting entrepreneurship and self-employment.
• Training modules will incorporate modern techniques alongside traditional skills, ensuring relevance in contemporary markets.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Skill Development
prelimsPoints: PM Vishwakarma scheme to expand.
Include more trades, enhanced financial support, skill training.
Focus on digital economy integration, market linkages.
Aims to preserve heritage, promote entrepreneurship.
Training to blend modern and traditional techniques.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of supporting traditional artisans and craftspeople for cultural preservation and economic empowerment in India.
Analyze the objectives and impact of the PM Vishwakarma scheme in promoting skill development and entrepreneurship.
Evaluate the challenges faced by traditional artisans in a modern economy and how policy interventions can address them.
Examine the role of market linkages and digital integration in making artisanal products competitive globally.
GS2.imageDescription: An Indian artisan meticulously crafting a traditional item, symbolizing heritage and skill.
GS2.trivia: India's handicraft sector is one of the largest employment generators after agriculture, supporting millions of artisans.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate, Reiterates Focus on Inflation Targeting Amid Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized the MPC's commitment to bringing inflation within the target band of 2-6%, with a medium-term target of 4%.
• The decision comes against a backdrop of persistent global supply chain disruptions and volatile crude oil prices, posing upside risks to inflation.
• RBI revised its FY26 GDP growth projection slightly downwards to 6.8% from 7.0%, citing global slowdown concerns.
• Liquidity management measures will continue to ensure adequate credit flow to productive sectors while managing inflationary pressures.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate remains unchanged at 6.5%.
RBI's inflation target is 2-6%, with a medium-term goal of 4%.
FY26 GDP growth projection revised to 6.8%.
MPC considers global supply chain and crude oil volatility.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflation.
Evaluate the impact of global economic headwinds on India's monetary policy decisions and growth outlook.
Examine the role of the MPC in maintaining financial stability.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with its logo, symbolizing monetary policy decisions.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: Union Cabinet Approves New National Policy on Sustainable Urban Planning and Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet today approved the 'National Policy on Sustainable Urban Planning and Development (NPSUPD) 2025'.
• The policy aims to promote green building standards, efficient public transport systems, and integrated waste management in urban areas.
• It mandates the adoption of climate-resilient infrastructure and encourages the use of renewable energy in urban projects.
• Emphasis is placed on citizen participation in urban governance and leveraging technology for smart city solutions.
• The policy targets reducing the carbon footprint of Indian cities by 20% by 2035 and enhancing liveability scores.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: NPSUPD 2025 approved by Union Cabinet.
Aims for green building, public transport, waste management.
Mandates climate-resilient infrastructure.
Targets 20% carbon footprint reduction by 2035.
Emphasizes citizen participation and smart city tech.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges of rapid urbanization in India and the need for sustainable urban planning.
Discuss the key features of the NPSUPD 2025 and its potential to transform Indian cities.
Evaluate the role of technology and citizen participation in achieving sustainable urban development goals.
Examine the linkages between urban planning, climate change mitigation, and economic growth.
GS3.imageDescription: Modern cityscape with green spaces and sustainable transport, representing smart urban planning.
GS3.trivia: By 2050, it is estimated that India's urban population will grow by an additional 400 million people, making sustainable planning crucial.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Next-Gen Satellite for Enhanced Disaster Management and Early Warning
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched a new-generation geo-imaging satellite, 'EOS-08' (Earth Observation Satellite-08).
• EOS-08 is equipped with advanced synthetic aperture radar (SAR) and high-resolution optical sensors for precise data collection.
• It will significantly enhance India's capabilities in disaster management, providing real-time data for flood mapping, cyclone tracking, and landslide monitoring.
• The satellite is also designed for agricultural assessment, urban planning, and coastal zone management.
• This mission underscores India's growing self-reliance in space technology and its commitment to utilizing space assets for societal benefit.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: ISRO launched 'EOS-08'.
EOS-08 is a geo-imaging satellite.
Equipped with SAR and optical sensors.
Enhances disaster management, flood mapping, cyclone tracking.
Also used for agriculture, urban planning, coastal zone management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of space technology in disaster management and mitigation efforts in India.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in remote sensing contribute to various sectors like agriculture and urban development.
Evaluate India's progress in achieving self-reliance in space technology and its geopolitical implications.
Examine the challenges in integrating satellite data into ground-level disaster response mechanisms.
GS3.imageDescription: An ISRO rocket launching into space, with the Earth visible in the background, symbolizing satellite deployment.
GS3.trivia: India's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on April 19, 1975, by the Soviet Union.

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission: Progress Report Highlights Key Achievements and Future Roadmap
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy released a progress report on the National Green Hydrogen Mission, highlighting significant strides.
• India has attracted over $5 billion in investments for green hydrogen projects, primarily in Gujarat and Karnataka.
• The report notes the successful commissioning of pilot projects for green hydrogen production and its application in industrial sectors like refineries.
• Challenges remain in reducing the cost of green hydrogen production and developing robust storage and transportation infrastructure.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030, targeting 5 million tonnes per annum capacity.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission progress report released.
Attracted over $5 billion investment.
Pilot projects commissioned in Gujarat, Karnataka.
Challenges: cost reduction, storage, transport.
Aim: 5 MTPA capacity by 2030, India as global hub.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic viability and technological challenges associated with scaling up green hydrogen production.
Evaluate the potential of the National Green Hydrogen Mission to transform India into an export hub for green energy.
Examine the policy support and investment required to achieve the mission's ambitious targets.
GS3.imageDescription: Stylized image of a hydrogen molecule forming from green energy sources like wind turbines and solar panels.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all normal matter.

GS3.headline: Comprehensive Border Management System (CBMS) Deployment in Sensitive Sectors Shows Positive Results
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs announced the successful deployment of the Comprehensive Border Management System (CBMS) in several sensitive border sectors.
• CBMS integrates advanced surveillance technologies, including drones, thermal imagers, ground sensors, and real-time data analytics.
• The system aims to enhance border security, prevent infiltration, and curb smuggling activities more effectively.
• Initial reports indicate a significant reduction in cross-border incidents and improved response times for border guarding forces.
• The government plans to expand CBMS coverage to all critical land and coastal borders over the next five years.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: CBMS deployed in sensitive border sectors.
Integrates drones, thermal imagers, ground sensors.
Aims to enhance border security and prevent infiltration.
Initial reports show reduction in cross-border incidents.
Planned expansion to all critical borders.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of border security challenges for India and the need for technological solutions.
Analyze the components and effectiveness of the Comprehensive Border Management System (CBMS).
Evaluate the balance between technology deployment and human intelligence in securing India's borders.
Examine the geopolitical implications of enhanced border security on regional stability.
GS3.imageDescription: High-tech border fence with surveillance cameras and drones patrolling, symbolizing modern border management.
GS3.trivia: India has over 15,000 km of land border and 7,500 km of coastline, making border management a complex task.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Proposes National AI Ethics Framework for Responsible AI Development
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog today released a discussion paper proposing a comprehensive National AI Ethics Framework for India.
• The framework emphasizes principles of fairness, accountability, transparency, safety, and privacy in the development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence.
• It aims to foster responsible AI innovation while mitigating potential risks such as bias, discrimination, and job displacement.
• The framework suggests a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and civil society for its implementation.
• This move positions India as a leader in developing ethical guidelines for AI, balancing technological progress with societal well-being.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog proposed National AI Ethics Framework.
Emphasizes fairness, accountability, transparency, safety, privacy.
Aims for responsible AI innovation and risk mitigation.
Suggests multi-stakeholder approach.
Positions India as a leader in AI ethics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical dilemmas and societal implications posed by the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence.
Analyze the key principles of NITI Aayog's proposed AI Ethics Framework and its relevance in the Indian context.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a comprehensive AI ethics framework, considering technological advancements and diverse stakeholder interests.
Examine how India can balance AI innovation with ethical governance to ensure inclusive growth.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract image showing a human brain connected to digital circuits, symbolizing AI and ethical considerations.
GS3.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined in 1956 by John McCarthy at the Dartmouth Conference.

GS3.headline: Coastal Zone Management Plan Implementation Reviewed: Focus on Resilience and Livelihoods
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change reviewed the progress of Coastal Zone Management Plans (CZMPs) implementation.
• The review highlighted efforts in protecting critical coastal ecosystems like mangroves and coral reefs, and mitigating coastal erosion.
• Challenges include balancing developmental activities with environmental conservation and ensuring the participation of local fishing communities.
• New guidelines are proposed to integrate climate change adaptation measures, such as seawalls and early warning systems, into CZMPs.
• The plans aim to safeguard coastal livelihoods, promote sustainable tourism, and enhance disaster preparedness in coastal regions.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: CZMPs implementation reviewed by MoEFCC.
Focus on protecting mangroves, coral reefs, mitigating erosion.
Challenges: balancing development and conservation, community participation.
New guidelines for climate change adaptation.
Aims to safeguard livelihoods, promote sustainable tourism, disaster preparedness.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of India's coastal zones.
Analyze the challenges in implementing Coastal Zone Management Plans, particularly regarding stakeholder conflicts and climate change impacts.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current policies in protecting coastal ecosystems and ensuring sustainable livelihoods.
Suggest measures for enhancing coastal resilience and integrating local communities in conservation efforts.
GS3.imageDescription: A healthy mangrove forest along a coastline, illustrating coastal ecosystem protection.
GS3.trivia: The Sundarbans, shared between India and Bangladesh, is the largest single block of tidal halophytic mangrove forest in the world.

GS3.headline: India on Track to Exceed 2030 Renewable Energy Target, Ministry Report Claims
GS3.explainer:
• A report by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy indicates that India is well on its way to exceeding its 2030 renewable energy targets.
• The country has already achieved over 180 GW of installed renewable capacity, including large hydro, surpassing initial projections.
• Aggressive solar and wind energy deployment, coupled with policy incentives, have been key drivers of this growth.
• The report highlights the role of schemes like the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) for solar PV manufacturing in boosting domestic production.
• While grid integration and storage remain challenges, India is poised to become a global leader in renewable energy transition.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: India on track to exceed 2030 renewable energy targets.
Over 180 GW installed renewable capacity achieved.
Solar and wind deployment, policy incentives are key drivers.
PLI scheme for solar PV manufacturing boosting domestic production.
Challenges: grid integration, storage.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's progress in renewable energy adoption and its implications for energy security and climate change mitigation.
Analyze the effectiveness of government policies and schemes, such as PLI, in accelerating the renewable energy transition.
Evaluate the challenges associated with integrating high shares of renewable energy into the national grid and potential solutions.
Examine India's potential to emerge as a global leader in renewable energy technology and deployment.
GS3.imageDescription: Vast solar panel farm stretching into the horizon, symbolizing renewable energy generation.
GS3.trivia: The International Solar Alliance (ISA) was conceived as a joint effort by India and France to mobilize efforts against climate change through solar energy.

GS3.headline: Government to Launch Phase 2 of Revamped PM-KISAN Scheme with Enhanced Benefits
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Agriculture Ministry announced plans to launch Phase 2 of the revamped Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme.
• The second phase will introduce enhanced financial assistance for small and marginal farmers, including linkage with crop insurance and credit facilities.
• It aims to streamline beneficiary identification through Aadhaar and land record integration, minimizing leakages and ensuring timely disbursals.
• The revamped scheme will also focus on promoting sustainable agricultural practices and farmer producer organizations (FPOs).
• This initiative is expected to further boost farmer incomes and contribute to agricultural sector resilience.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country
Different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
E-technology in the aid of farmers
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
prelimsPoints: Phase 2 of revamped PM-KISAN scheme to be launched.
Enhanced financial assistance, linkage with crop insurance, credit.
Streamlined beneficiary identification via Aadhaar and land records.
Focus on sustainable agriculture and FPOs.
Aims to boost farmer incomes and resilience.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and impact of the PM-KISAN scheme on farmer welfare and agricultural productivity in India.
Analyze the effectiveness of direct benefit transfers (DBT) in reducing leakages and improving last-mile delivery of agricultural subsidies.
Evaluate the role of technology (Aadhaar, land records) in enhancing the efficiency and transparency of farmer support schemes.
Examine how linking financial assistance with sustainable practices and FPOs can transform the agricultural sector.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer working in a field with modern agricultural tools, symbolizing farmer welfare schemes.
GS3.trivia: PM-KISAN was launched on 24 February 2019, providing income support to all eligible farmer families across the country.

GS3.headline: New Foreign Trade Policy Unveiled, Aims to Boost Exports to $2 Trillion by 2030
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry unveiled the new Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2025-30, replacing the previous policy.
• The policy sets an ambitious target of achieving $2 trillion in exports of goods and services by 2030.
• Key features include simplified procedures, automation of processes, and a focus on emerging sectors like green energy and e-commerce exports.
• It introduces new incentive schemes and rationalizes existing ones to promote 'Make in India' products globally.
• The FTP also emphasizes district-as-export-hubs initiative and strengthening India's position in global value chains.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: New Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2025-30 unveiled.
Target: $2 trillion exports by 2030.
Features: simplified procedures, automation, focus on green energy, e-commerce.
New incentive schemes, rationalized existing ones.
Emphasizes 'district-as-export-hubs' and global value chains.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and key features of India's new Foreign Trade Policy 2025-30.
Discuss the strategies outlined in the policy to achieve the ambitious export target of $2 trillion by 2030.
Evaluate the potential impact of promoting 'Make in India' products and strengthening global value chains on India's economic growth.
Examine the role of e-commerce and district-level initiatives in diversifying and boosting India's export basket.
GS3.imageDescription: A container ship loading at a port, with global trade routes overlaid, symbolizing international commerce.
GS3.trivia: India's merchandise exports crossed the $400 billion mark for the first time in FY22.
